summary: R2 fixes are implemented. Same-district postwar arrangements are now rejected regardless of settlementId by using the district administrative postwar marker. Direct control after an existing vassal or tribute-only postwar arrangement is rejected with the same stable duplicate reason instead of leaving stale suzerain or obligation rights. restore-vassal-ruler now requires concrete local ruler and policy inputs and validates that the ruler and policy actually fit the restored district.

tests_run.result: R2 red pass before the fix: different-settlementId same-district duplicate, direct-after-vassal, and null ruler/policy restore cases were accepted.

decisions: R2 conflict rule: a district can have only one postwar governance arrangement for the victor marker. Repeated same-district commands are rejected with duplicate-obligation-settlement at payload.districtId, regardless of settlementId or method.

Direct-after-vassal/tribute replacement is rejected for M3 instead of clearing prior rights. This is the smallest M3-safe rule and avoids introducing a treaty replacement or rights-transfer model before M4.

The duplicate guard is district-scoped through the administrative district marker produced by the postwar command path; it does not reject a separate district solely because the same victor/local polities already have postwar obligations elsewhere.

restore-vassal-ruler requires non-null localRulerCharacterId and policyId. The command also validates the ruler exists, is available in the district, belongs to the local polity, and that the policy exists and targets the restored district.
Regression coverage now asserts different-settlementId same-district duplicate rejection, direct-after-vassal rejection, and null ruler/policy restore rejection with stable reason codes.
risks: R3 public protocol surface remains changed from the original M3 implementation and still needs systems_architect review for long-term command/query naming and compatibility.

The replacement/clearing path is intentionally not implemented. If design later needs postwar arrangement replacement, systems_architect should define the deterministic rights and obligation clearing semantics before implementation.
Projection coefficients remain prototype constants for M3 validation and are not final balance.
requested_action: Review the R2 conflict handling, restore-vassal-ruler validation, and regression coverage. Confirm whether the M3-safe reject-on-existing-arrangement rule is acceptable.
